section_title: INTRODUCTION AND WEEKLY FAIR VALUE GAP REVIEW FOR ES
content:
Welcome back. This presentation will quickly go over concepts discussed, especially considering the
current thunderstorm. It's recommended to review the market review from last Friday, June 23rd, 2023, as
the topics discussed here were forecasted before they appeared on the chart.
Observing the weekly Candlestick chart, you can see that the market did fill the fair value gap, which is a
sell-side imbalance, buy-side inefficiency. This was achieved with an overshoot and wick. The buy side was
then worked above. As mentioned on Twitter and in the market review, this area was likely where ES would
be drawn to. This presentation will primarily focus on ES. You can find a link in the description to watch a
live trade of both ES and NASDAQ from this morning.

section_title: DAILY CHART ANALYSIS AND PRE-MARKET FORECAST (JUNE 23RD TWEET)
content:
Zooming in on a daily chart, on June 23rd, a tweet was shared at 9:00 AM New York local time. The tweet,
which remains unedited and undeleted, plainly stated a preference for the lower of two fair value gaps on
ES. This preference was also observed in NASDAQ charts. Both fair value gaps were subsequently filled
today, as noted in the chart posts.
In the market review on Friday, June 23rd, 2023, a slide for ES showed an 'eyeball icon' indicating the
forecasted drawdown into approximately 4369-4370. The hourly chart confirms that price did indeed trade
down into this target perfectly today.
15
- MINUTE ANALYSIS:

section_title: SIBI AND BUY-SIDE DELIVERY DYNAMICS
content:
Moving to the 15-minute chart for a more detailed look, we observe a fair value gap in the form of a sell
- side imbalance, buy-side inefficiency (SIBI), with only a small area remaining. When the market traded up
to a certain point, it did not completely reprice to that level, leaving an exposed area of inefficiency.
Since it's a SIBI, indicative of buy-side delivery inefficiency, the market is likely to draw back up into that
area with upward motion, meaning buy-side delivery. Correspondingly, buy-side liquidity or buy stops would
be resting just above this inefficiency.

section_title: NEW WEEK OPENING GAP, WEEKLY FVG, AND RETAIL TRAPS
content:
The new week opening gap high and low are visible, alongside the high and low of the weekly fair value
gap. Initially, price ran up to take the buy side, attempting to fully reprice to the SIBI high. All of this initial
upward movement was a 'sucker's play', trapping retail traders who bought into it. Unless they were nimble
enough to exit, they were caught off guard and offside.
Subsequently, the market repriced down to the target level that was forecasted last Friday. Note the clear
utilization of the new week opening gap in this price action.
5
- MINUTE CHART:

section_title: MIDNIGHT OPENING PRICE AND 9:30 AM RALLY
content:
On the five-minute chart, we delve into the core of today's price delivery. The New York midnight opening
price, which is the opening price of a specific candle, is extended throughout the day until 11:00 AM
according to the rules provided. Price delivery is evident as the market rallies at 9:30 AM.
This rally trades up to the buy-side liquidity pool that was identified on the 15-minute timeframe, specifically
inside that 15-minute fair value gap SIBI. This area and its high correspond to the shaded region discussed
previously.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC REPRICING TO PREMIUM AND OPTIMAL SHORT ENTRY CONDITIONS
content:
The New York opening price is a critical marker. The algorithm will reprice to a premium, not to some
random level, but specifically to an old inefficiency. As per previous lectures, the best shorts occur above
the New York midnight opening price. The market rallied up multiple times to these levels.
A common question arises: 'Why are these specific highs chosen for the buy-side liquidity pool?' The
selection of these highs is not arbitrary, but based on the principles taught in the 'One Setup for Life' lecture
from last week.

section_title: 'ONE SETUP FOR LIFE' LECTURE AND LIQUIDITY IDENTIFICATION
content:
The 'One Setup for Life' lecture was designed to teach you how to find setups that will serve you
perpetually, ensuring you never run out of trading opportunities. It emphasizes identifying specific liquidity
targets to aim for. The lecture is not an entry pattern itself, but a framework for discovering consistent
setups.
Recalling the forecast from last Friday at 9:00 AM New York local time, before the market even opened, the
target for ES was set at 43.70, or just below it at 43.69. This specific target was communicated to those
following on Twitter.

section_title: ALGORITHMIC PRICING, JUDAS SWING, AND INSTITUTIONAL LOGIC
content:
This is precisely where the algorithm was projected to price ES, with no edits or corrections made to the
original forecast. The entire run-up in price is categorized as a 'Judas Swing' a false run designed to
entice people to chase it, getting them offside. It targets a very specific price array, serving as the element
for entering a sell program.
This move is driven by supply and demand zones, not harmonic patterns, Elliott wave, or Wyckoff analysis.

section_title: NEW WEEK OPENING GAP AND LONDON SESSION LIQUIDITY
content:
The attention last Friday was called to this specific level. Following the weekend, the market opened and
created the new week opening gap. Subsequently, the market rallied through this gap to specific levels.
This involved running out buy-side liquidity, identified as London session liquidity, and then moving towards
the low of the London session, which represents London session sell-side liquidity.

section_title: OPENING RANGE JUDAS SWING AND SMART MONEY SHORTING STRATEGY
content:
At 9:30 AM, the market opened and rallied, drawing in retail traders who anticipated higher prices. This
rally, however, was a Judas Swing, moving into a specific level above the New York midnight opening price.
This is exactly where the highest probability shorts are expected to form for a bearish bias.
During the 9:30-10:00 AM window, which is the opening range, this rally is observed as being opposed to
the bearish direction forecasted last Friday. While retail perceives this as an upward trend, smart money
utilizes it as an opportunity to initiate short positions.

section_title: DEFINING THE OPENING RANGE GAP (ORG)
content:
- PART 1
The market action utilized counterparty London session liquidity, aiming for month-end session liquidity.
The focus now shifts to understanding the 'Opening Range Gap' (ORG). To identify this, on your
TradingView chart, ensure 'regular trading hours' is toggled, not 'electronic trading hours'.
On a one-minute chart, for Friday's data, hover over the last candle at 4:14 PM. The closing price of this
candle, which was 4388 even on Friday, June 23rd, 2023, is the first key level.

section_title: DEFINING THE OPENING RANGE GAP (ORG)
content:
- PART 2: DISCOUNT AND PREMIUM
After identifying Friday's closing price, wait until 9:30 AM New York local time on Monday, June 26th, 2023,
when the market begins regular trading hours. The opening price at 9:30 AM on Monday is the second key
level.
The difference between Friday's closing price and Monday's opening price defines the Opening Range Gap
(ORG).
- If the market opens lower than Friday's close, it's an 'opening range gap lower', or a 'discount opening
range gap'.
- If the market opens higher than Friday's close, it's an 'opening range gap higher', or an 'opening range
gap premium'.

section_title: CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT VS. MEAN THRESHOLD
content:
It's important to distinguish between two types of midpoints:
- Consequent Encroachment: This refers to the midpoint of any gap or wick. Any gap's midpoint is always
utilized as consequent encroachment.
- Mean Threshold: This refers to the midpoint of any order block (breaker block, bullish or bearish order
block, propulsion block). It represents 50 percent of that range.
These are two distinct viewpoints for defining a midpoint.
ONE
- MINUTE CHART APPLICATION:

section_title: JUDAS SWING AND PREMIUM SIBI
content:
With the Opening Range Gap (ORG) defined, let's apply this information to the ES one-minute chart for
September 2023 delivery. The rally observed at 9:30 AM is a Judas Swing, running above buy-side liquidity
established in the London session. This would be visible with electronic trading hours.
The market rallies up into a premium, specifically a fair value gap in the form of a SIBI, and also into buy
- side liquidity. You may need to review this video multiple times to keep track of the different timeframes.
Critically, price is trading above the New York midnight opening price, which is a key reference level.

section_title: MARKET STRUCTURE SHIFT AND FVG ENTRY CONSIDERATIONS
content:
A definitive drop is observed where previous lows were taken out, followed by a fair value gap. One could
have used this fair value gap for entry, but it would require placing a stop loss above the short-term high to
account for it. This specific entry was not preferred.
Price then ran up once more before breaking down. Although a shift in market structure occurred below a
previous low, which could have been used for entry, the crucial factor was the price running one more time
above the New York midnight opening price and back inside the fair value gap SIBI.

section_title: ADVANCED STOP LOSS PLACEMENT WITH MULTIPLE GAPS
content:
This final run into a premium preceded a breakdown. Two gaps were present: one shaded in yellow and
another. In the accompanying recording, a measurement is taken from a candle's low to another candle's
high to find its midpoint, or 50 percent, which is the consequent encroachment. The stop loss was placed
just above this consequent encroachment.
This strategy is employed because the market has algorithmically proven it's not going higher. When there
are two gaps, the 'two rule' dictates factoring in the higher gap for stop placement, determining the
acceptable drawdown without taking the full risk of the open.

section_title: ANTICIPATING ORG REPRICING MACRO AND TRADE ENTRY
content:
The stop loss was placed just above the consequent encroachment of the higher fair value gap, as
demonstrated in the trade execution. Even though a previous low wasn't taken out, it wasn't necessary for
entry.
Entry was made with the anticipation of the algorithm repricing back down into the opening range gap. This
entire sequence is referred to as a 'repricing macro'.

section_title: JUDAS SWING AND SILVER BULLET ENTRY STRATEGY
content:
All of this price action is considered a Judas Swing. Due to external commitments, active trade
management was not possible, so a simplified approach using the Silver Bullet rules was applied. This
involves identifying the low to the high, finding equilibrium, and looking for a discount array below 50
percent or below a specific level where a gap exists.
The trade aimed for the opening range gap, entering into a discount using an imbalance, and targeting a
run below the new week opening gap low. This approach is evident in the trade executions.

section_title: EXECUTION AND PYRAMIDING STRATEGY
content:
The trade involved shorting six contracts at 43.95 and a half, and 43.94 and a half, aligning with the
concept of premium running to discount. If active day trading management were possible, additional entries
(pyramiding) would have been taken, as highlighted in the review video showing a limit order at a fair value
gap.
These additional entries would have aimed for the lower end target at 43.70. Without active management,
these specific entries would still have yielded significant profits. For instance, dual short trades on ES and
NASDAQ, as demonstrated live, would have resulted in an approximately $16,000 profit, even without
capturing the full price run.
ONE
- MINUTE CHART:

section_title: ORG, JUDAS SWING, AND STOP PLACEMENT REFINEMENT
content:
Zooming in on the one-minute chart, we see the opening range gap and the Judas Swing upwards into buy
- side liquidity. Friday's New York session liquidity was an additional factor considered, which influenced the
decision to wait for a specific entry. While an earlier short entry might have been viable with a stop above,
the presence of two fair value gaps was critical.
To enter the preferred trade, 50 percent of the fair value gap's range (consequent encroachment) was
used, with the stop placed above it, as seen in the execution. Price then dropped, rallied back into a SIBI,
and traded up into it once more.

section_title: POST-ENTRY PRICE ACTION AND NEW WEEK OPENING GAP TARGET
content:
If the limit order had remained active and limit exits were not taken, it would have presented an opportunity
for an additional pyramiding entry. This would necessitate holding the position throughout the lunch
session, anticipating a run back into the New Week Opening Gap, which was ultimately achieved with
perfect consequent encroachment.
All of the initial run-up is identified as the Judas Swing. While it would have been valid to trade aspects of
this move by getting short or selling long positions, the primary objective, based on the Friday forecast of a
43.70 target for ES, was to seek short opportunities.

section_title: JUDAS SWING AND LIQUIDITY TARGETS FOR SHORT BIAS
content:
The ideal scenario for shorts involved price running into specific liquidity. Two forms of buy-side liquidity
were targeted:
- London session buy side
- Friday's New York session buy side
It is encouraged to review this presentation again, annotate your own charts, and observe how these
elements precisely align with the trade rationale.

section_title: SILVER BULLET STRATEGY AND DUAL TRADE PROFITABILITY
content:
The trade was executed using the Silver Bullet mentality, specifically targeting a return into the opening
range gap, and more precisely, a run below the new week opening gap low. It's important to note that it's
not necessary to capture the entire price run to achieve substantial profits. As demonstrated with dual
trades on both ES and NASDAQ, taking these same trades could have resulted in a $16,000 increase in a
funded or live account, even without holding for the full move.

section_title: NEW YORK LUNCH LIQUIDITY AND TARGET RE-EVALUATION
content:
In the 'One Setup for Life' lecture, the focus is on liquidity, especially for a bearish bias. While the price did
not immediately reach the 43.70 level, it's crucial to identify other liquidity points. The New York lunch buy
- side liquidity, marked by highs around noon and extending into the opening range gap (shaded area),
presents a logical reason for a potential rally.
It makes perfect sense for price to reach into half of that yellow shaded area (consequent encroachment).
This understanding benefits those who knew price was likely heading to 43.70, enabling them to make
advantageous trades.

section_title: SMART MONEY STRATEGY AND MARKET ON CLOSE ALGORITHM
content:
Smart money strategies involve waiting for the algorithm to reprice back up into the midpoint (consequent
encroachment) of the shaded area (Opening Range Gap) or just above relative equal highs. The algorithm
quickly reaches this level, then consolidates until the 3:15-3:45 PM 'Market on Close' algorithm activates.
From 3:15 to 3:45 PM, the algorithm drives price precisely to the 43.70 target, which was forecasted last
Friday at 9:00 AM.

section_title: THE POWER OF 'ONE SETUP FOR LIFE'
content:
- PERPETUAL TRADE OPPORTUNITIES
The final move attacked the opposing side's sell-side liquidity of the lunch hour. Revisit the 'One Setup for
Life' lecture; the logic taught there is consistently applied in all trades and executions shown over the years.
Many have asked to learn liquidity, bias, and market direction, believing it's the only missing piece.
While other concepts are important, the 'One Setup for Life' lecture provides the foundational
understanding to:
- Never run out of setups.
- Consistently impress with your trading results.
- Successfully pass funded accounts.
- Profitably trade in live accounts.
- Dominate any market, asset class, or timeframe.

section_title: STUDY AND LIVE TRADE EXECUTION (ES & NASDAQ)
content:
It is encouraged not to simply take these words at face value but to study the concepts diligently. If you
enjoyed this video, please give a thumbs up, as it's a free way to offer encouragement. Additionally, click
the link in the video description, above the disclaimer.
